rag_rules: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations and nested if-then-else for branching logic in AGREE contracts.
Place component type contracts in component type scopes and implementation bindings in implementation scopes.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use logical operators (and, or, not, =>) and follow syntax: assume/guarantee with labeled expressions.
Constants and equations in AGREE should use valid scalar types: int, real, bool.
Visible identifiers from AADL model must be used in AGREE expressions; avoid AADL property names as variables.
Component type annexes should only reference visible ports and features, not implementation-only subcomponents.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Assumptions and guarantees must be labeled and use valid AGREE expression syntax.
Equations and constants must declare correct types (int, real, bool) matching AADL feature types.
anti_patterns: Do not reference implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts.
Do not encode AADL timing properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables unless explicitly exposed.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and unit tokens (ms, Hz, Bytes) are not valid in AGREE scope.
Model fusion and validation repair patterns are not relevant to AGREE annex generation.
